The **Clube de Regatas do Flamengo Youth Academy** ([Brazilian Portuguese](/wiki/Brazilian_Portuguese_language "Brazilian Portuguese language"): *Categorias de Base*) are the youth academy of [Clube de Regatas do Flamengo](/wiki/Clube_de_Regatas_do_Flamengo "Clube de Regatas do Flamengo"), a Brazilian football club based in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro").[[2]](#cite_note-2) Is composed of several youth teams and is considered one of the most prolific football academies in [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") as also in the world.

Flamengo's Youth Squads of all categories have won trophies at national and international level. Numerous international players have graduated from the academy team. Notable academy graduates in recent years include [Lyon](/wiki/Olympique_Lyonnais "Olympique Lyonnais") midfielder [Lucas Paquetá](/wiki/Lucas_Paquet%C3%A1 "Lucas Paquetá"), [Real Madrid](/wiki/Real_Madrid_CF "Real Madrid CF") forward [Vinícius Júnior](/wiki/Vin%C3%ADcius_J%C3%BAnior "Vinícius Júnior"), [2016 Summer Olympic Games](/wiki/Football_at_the_2016_Summer_Olympics "Football at the 2016 Summer Olympics") Gold Medal midfielder [Renato Augusto](/wiki/Renato_Augusto "Renato Augusto") alongside many first team players, such as midfielder [Reinier Jesus](/wiki/Reinier_Jesus "Reinier Jesus").

The youth sector is composed of various squads divided by age groups. Clube de Regatas do Flamengo is responsible for over 100 young athletes in 5 different categories: U-11, U-13, U-15, U-17, U-20. The U-20 squad currently plays in the [Campeonato Brasileiro Sub-20](/wiki/Campeonato_Brasileiro_Sub-20 "Campeonato Brasileiro Sub-20"), the [Copa do Brasil Sub-20](/wiki/Copa_do_Brasil_Sub-20 "Copa do Brasil Sub-20"), the [Copa São Paulo de Futebol Júnior](/wiki/Copa_S%C3%A3o_Paulo_de_Futebol_J%C3%BAnior "Copa São Paulo de Futebol Júnior"), the Campeonato Carioca Sub-20 and the Torneio Octávio Pinto Guimarães.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] The U-17 squad currently plays in the Campeonato Brasileiro Sub-17, the [Copa do Brasil Sub-17](/wiki/Copa_do_Brasil_Sub-17 "Copa do Brasil Sub-17") and the Campeonato Carioca Sub-17.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Stadium

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CR_Flamengo_(youth)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Stadium")]

Main article: [Estádio da Gávea](/wiki/Est%C3%A1dio_da_G%C3%A1vea "Estádio da Gávea")

Flamengo's Youth Academy home stadium is nominally the **Estádio da Gávea** (officially named the **Estádio José Bastos Padilha** at Flamengo's Gávea Headquarters), which was inaugurated on September 4, 1938, and has a capacity of 4,000 people.[[3]](#cite_note-3) The stadium is named after José Bastos Padilha, Flamengo's president at the time of the stadium's construction, from 1933 to 1937.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Gávea Stadium is not actually located in the neighborhood of [Gávea](/wiki/G%C3%A1vea "Gávea") but rather in [Leblon](/wiki/Leblon "Leblon").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Since the 1990s, the stadium has been used almost exclusively for the club's youth and women's teams' matches, and as the training ground for the senior team.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Ninho do Urubu

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=CR_Flamengo_(youth)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ninho do Urubu")]

Main article: [Ninho do Urubu](/wiki/Ninho_do_Urubu "Ninho do Urubu")

All the youth teams currently train at the club's main training ground, [Ninho do Urubu](/wiki/Ninho_do_Urubu "Ninho do Urubu"), located in the [Vargem Grande](/wiki/Vargem_Grande,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Vargem Grande, Rio de Janeiro") neighborhood, in the [West Zone](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro#West_Zone "Rio de Janeiro").[[7]](#cite_note-7) These athletes have modern dormitories, living room, recreation room and cafeteria. The athletes also have medical, dental and psychological assistance.

On the morning of February 8, 2019 a fire broke out in the living quarters of several youth academy players while they were sleeping.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Ten players between the ages of 14 and 17 were killed, and three others were hospitalized with burn injuries.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
